things.name: National Museum of Beirut
things.description: Lebanon's finest archaeological collection spanning Phoenician, Roman and Byzantine eras

things.name: Jeita Grotto
things.description: Spectacular system of interconnected limestone caves with the world's largest stalactite

things.name: Downtown Beirut (Central District)
things.description: Restored Ottoman and French Mandate architecture around Martyrs' Square and Nejmeh Square

things.name: Baalbek Roman Temples
things.description: UNESCO World Heritage largest Roman temples ever built - the Temple of Jupiter is the biggest

things.name: Gemmayzeh & Mar Mikhael
things.description: Beirut's most vibrant street art and nightlife neighbourhoods with legendary bar scene

things.name: Corniche Promenade
things.description: Seaside promenade from Raouche Rocks to the lighthouse, beloved for evening strolls

things.name: Sursock Museum
things.description: Lebanese contemporary art in a beautifully restored 1912 Italian-style mansion

things.name: Byblos (Jbeil)
things.description: UNESCO World Heritage Phoenician city - possibly the oldest continuously inhabited city in the world

things.name: Hamra Street
things.description: Beirut's cosmopolitan street with bookshops, cafes and the cultural pulse of West Beirut

things.name: Mleeta Resistance Tourist Landmark
things.description: Hezbollah museum in the hills showing military equipment from the resistance to Israeli occupation
